title: NBA Insider explains why a Jaylen Brown for Giannis trade would be ‘very simple’ for both sides
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics "Of course Milwaukee would see some of the stuff that Jaylen Brown is saying and they would get ready to go and call Boston" Milwaukee Bucks' Giannis Antetokounmpo gets past Boston Celtics' Jaylen Brown during the second half of an NBA basketball game Tuesday, April 9, 2024, in Milwaukee.
The Bucks won 104-91.

(AP Photo/Morry Gash) Rumors of the Celtics’ interest in trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo have been swirling for months, and the Celtics would have to move a significant amount of salary in order to make it happen.

title: Worcester man fatally stabbed near NYC’s Times Square identified
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Crime Leonides Baez, of Worcester, died of his injuries at the hospital Monday, police said.
The 39-year-old fatally stabbed near Times Square in New York City Monday night has been identified as a Worcester man, police said.

Leonides Baez, with a Main Street address in Worcester, died of his injuries at the hospital Monday, a spokesperson for the New York City Police Department’s Deputy Commissioner, Public Information said.

title: Mighty Squirrel Brewing Co. is opening a massive beer garden in Waltham
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Beer The 60,000-square-foot outdoor spot opens May 8.
Brett Phelps/The Boston Globe Mighty Squirrel Brewing Co.
is trading the taproom for the lawn this summer with a sprawling beer garden at Gore Place.

title: City Council calls on BPS to explore joining nationwide litigation aimed at social media companies
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News Elected officials in Massachusetts are growing increasingly wary of social media companies and the allegedly addicting features they deploy.
Boston City Hall.
David L.

title: Where to watch the World Cup in Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: World Cup From Fenway sports bars to neighborhood pubs, find the best places to watch World Cup matches — plus a map to spots near you.
(Jessica Rinaldi/Globe Staff) May 6, 2026 | 4:07 PM 2 minutes to read You don’t need a ticket to Gillette to feel the World Cup energy this summer.
As teams and fans from around the world descend into Greater Boston, local bars and restaurants are planning their own game-day action with watch parties, specials, and sound-on screenings.

title: 5 takeaways from Brad Stevens’s end of the year press conference
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics "One of the things we've got to figure out is how to have more of an impact at the rim and I think we do need to add to our team to do that.
" Brad Stevens speaking at his end-of-season press conference on May 6, 2026.
David L Ryan/Globe Staff ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Apartment availability in Boston climbs to the highest level in years
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News More units, shifting student demand, and economic uncertainty are reshaping the rental market, with monthly prices still climbing.
An apartment building in Brighton.
Taylor Coester for The Boston Globe ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Wellesley mother pleads not guilty to killing her two children, is ordered held without bail
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime Janette MacAusland was ordered held without bail in the deaths of 6-year-old Ella and 7-year-old Kai MacAusland.
Wellesley mother Janette MacAusland appeared in Dedham District Court in connection with the deaths of her two children.
She wept as she left the courtroom.

title: Rescued, underweight Cane Corso mix to be ready for adoption this weekend after swift recovery
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News Balboa will be ready for his forever home after getting neutered Friday.
Balboa, a Cane Corso mix rescued in Dorchester, was brought in to MSPCA-Angell's emergency room (left) and has since been recovering and gaining weight (right).
Courtesy of MSPCA-Angell A then-severely underweight stray dog rescued in Dorchester is expected to be ready for adoption this weekend, significantly sooner than originally anticipated by caretakers.
